name: SQL Injection in Login
additional_info: CWE-89: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an SQL Command
category: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an SQL Command
description: A SQL injection vulnerability in the login flow occurs when user-supplied credentials are incorporated into authentication queries without proper neutralization, allowing attackers to alter the query logic. This can enable bypass of authentication controls and issuance of valid session tokens, potentially leading to account takeover, unauthorized data access, and further exploitation of authenticated functionality.

name: SQL Injection in /api/transactions
additional_info: CWE-89: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an SQL Command
category: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an SQL Command
description: A SQL injection vulnerability exists in the /api/transactions endpoint where user-supplied input is incorporated into database queries without proper parameterization or sanitization. An attacker can manipulate the resulting SQL to bypass intended filters and access unauthorized transaction records or other database data, and may be able to modify or disrupt data depending on database permissions.

name: Weak JWT Secret (Token Forgery)
additional_info: CWE-347: Improper Verification of Cryptographic Signature
description: The application uses JWTs signed with an HMAC secret that is weak, exposed, or not properly validated during verification, allowing attackers to create or modify tokens and have them accepted as authentic. By forging JWT signatures and manipulating claims (e.g., identity or role attributes), an attacker can bypass authentication and authorization controls, leading to user impersonation, privilege escalation to administrative access, and unauthorized access to sensitive data and protected operations.

name: BOLA - Unauthenticated Account Balance Access (/check_balance/{account_number})
additional_info: CWE-862: Missing Authorization
description: A missing authorization (BOLA/IDOR) vulnerability occurs in the /check_balance/{account_number} endpoint that exposes sensitive account data based solely on a user-controlled identifier without verifying the requester’s authentication and ownership. This allows unauthenticated or unauthorized users to access other users’ account details (e.g., balances and associated identifiers), leading to confidentiality breaches and enabling further fraud or social engineering.

name: BOLA - Unauthenticated Account Transaction Access (/transactions/{account_number})
additional_info: CWE-862: Missing Authorization
description: The /transactions/{account_number} endpoint lacks proper authorization checks, allowing requests to access transaction records for arbitrary account identifiers without verifying the caller’s identity or rights. This broken object-level authorization (CWE-862) can enable attackers to enumerate account numbers and retrieve other users’ sensitive financial transaction histories, leading to confidentiality breaches and potential fraud-related intelligence gathering.

name: BOLA - Authenticated Cross-Account Transaction Access (/api/transactions)
additional_info: CWE-639: Authorization Bypass Through User-Controlled Key
description: An authorization bypass (BOLA/IDOR) exists in the authenticated transaction history functionality where the endpoint /api/transactions uses a user-supplied account identifier without reliably enforcing object-level ownership checks. As a result, an authenticated user can access transaction data belonging to other accounts, leading to unauthorized disclosure of sensitive financial information and enabling cross-account data enumeration.

name: BOLA - Virtual Card Transaction Cross-User Access (/api/virtual-cards/{card_id}/transactions)
additional_info: CWE-639: Authorization Bypass Through User-Controlled Key
description: The endpoint /api/virtual-cards/{card_id}/transactions is vulnerable to broken object-level authorization (BOLA/IDOR) because it relies on a user-controlled card_id without enforcing ownership checks. As a result, attackers may be able to access other users’ virtual card transaction data—potentially even without authentication—leading to sensitive financial information disclosure and enabling large-scale data enumeration.

name: BOLA - Unauthorized Virtual Card Freeze/Unfreeze (/api/virtual-cards/{card_id}/toggle-freeze)
additional_info: CWE-639: Authorization Bypass Through User-Controlled Key
description: The /api/virtual-cards/{card_id}/toggle-freeze endpoint relies on a user-supplied card_id to identify the target resource but fails to enforce ownership or proper authorization checks. This broken object level authorization (CWE-639) allows an authenticated attacker to freeze or unfreeze other users’ virtual cards, leading to unauthorized account actions, service disruption, and potential financial impact.

name: BOLA - Unauthorized Fund Transfer from Another User's Account (/transfer)
additional_info: CWE-639: Authorization Bypass Through User-Controlled Key
description: The /transfer endpoint is vulnerable to broken object level authorization where a user-controlled account identifier is accepted without verifying ownership against the authenticated user. This allows an authenticated attacker to initiate transfers debiting funds from another user’s account, leading to unauthorized transactions and financial loss.

name: Mass Assignment on /register
additional_info: CWE-915: Improperly Controlled Modification of Dynamically-Determined Object Attributes
category: Improperly Controlled Modification of Dynamically-Determined Object Attributes
description: Mass assignment on /register occurs when the endpoint blindly maps client-supplied parameters to account attributes, allowing attackers to set sensitive or privileged fields during signup. This can enable unauthorized privilege escalation (e.g., gaining administrative roles) and manipulation of restricted properties such as account balances, leading to full compromise of access controls and data integrity.

name: Admin Panel Unauthorized Data Access
additional_info: CWE-200: Exposure of Sensitive Information to an Unauthorized Actor
category: Exposure of Sensitive Information to an Unauthorized Actor
description: The endpoint /sup3r_s3cr3t_admin exposes a full user management listing containing sensitive data to actors who are not properly authorized to view it. Inadequate access control allows disclosure of user identifiers and financial/account details, enabling user enumeration, privacy violations, and facilitating targeted fraud or follow-on attacks.

name: PIN Disclosure in Forgot Password Endpoints
additional_info: CWE-640: Weak Password Recovery Mechanism for Forgotten Password
category: Weak Password Recovery Mechanism for Forgotten Password
description: The password recovery flow exposes the password reset PIN in responses from the forgot-password and reset-password endpoints (e.g., /api/v1/forgot-password), leaking a credential intended to be delivered out-of-band. This weakness allows unauthenticated attackers to obtain or confirm valid reset codes, enabling unauthorized password resets and potential account takeover.

name: Insufficient JWT Session Expiration
additional_info: CWE-613: Insufficient Session Expiration
description: JWT-based sessions are not properly expired or invalidated: tokens may be issued without enforceable expiration and can remain usable even after security events like password resets or re-logins. As a result, previously issued (including stale or expired) JWTs can continue to authorize access to protected endpoints, enabling persistent account compromise if a token is stolen or leaked.

name: Plaintext Password Storage
additional_info: CWE-256: Plaintext Storage of a Password
description: Plaintext Password Storage occurs when user passwords are stored or transmitted in a recoverable cleartext form rather than as securely salted, one-way hashes. If an attacker gains access to application responses, logs, or underlying data stores, they can immediately obtain usable credentials, enabling account takeover, privilege escalation, and credential reuse attacks against other services.

name: Verbose Error Messages Exposing Internal Information
additional_info: CWE-209: Generation of Error Message Containing Sensitive Information
category: Generation of Error Message Containing Sensitive Information
description: Verbose Error Messages Exposing Internal Information occurs when endpoitns return overly detailed error or debug responses (e.g., raw database errors, query fragments, stack/exception details, internal identifiers, or configuration data) when requests fail or include unexpected input. This information disclosure can aid attackers in reconnaissance by revealing implementation and data model details, which can be leveraged to more effectively target follow-on attacks such as injection, account enumeration, or privilege abuse.

name: Negative Amount Transfer Enables Reverse Fund Theft (/transfer)
additional_info: CWE-840: Business Logic Errors
description: The /transfer endpoint contains a business logic flaw that fails to validate transfer amounts, allowing negative values to be processed as valid transactions. This can invert the intended money flow, enabling an authenticated user to illicitly move funds from another account to their own while the system records the transfer as successful, undermining financial integrity and auditability.

name: Zero/Trivial/Self-Transfer Accepted Without Validation (/transfer)
additional_info: CWE-840: Business Logic Errors
description: The /transfer endpoint contains a business logic validation flaw that allows zero, trivial (sub-minimum), or self-referential transfers to be accepted and recorded as successful transactions even when no funds actually move. This can pollute audit trails and transaction histories, mislead users and downstream systems, and enable abuse of any features or controls that rely on successful transfer events or transaction counts.

name: Monetary Rounding/Precision Error Creates Funds (/transfer)
additional_info: CWE-682: Incorrect Calculation
description: The /transfer endpoint performs monetary calculations with improper rounding/precision, allowing fractional-cent values to be processed inconsistently. This can result in incorrect debits/credits where balances increase without a corresponding decrease (or vice versa), enabling creation or siphoning of funds and causing accounting integrity issues over repeated transactions.

name: Loan Endpoint Accepts Zero/Negative Amounts (/loan)
additional_info: CWE-840: Business Logic Errors
description: The /loan endpoint contains a business logic validation flaw that allows loan requests to be created with invalid monetary values such as zero or negative amounts. Accepting these nonsensical inputs can corrupt downstream loan processing workflows and data integrity, and may enable abuse or bypass of later-stage validation that assumes requests contain legitimate amounts.

name: No Upper Bound Loan Limit Validation
additional_info: CWE-840: Business Logic Errors
description: The loan request endpoint lacks an upper-bound validation on the requested loan amount, allowing clients to submit excessively large values that are still accepted as successful. This business logic flaw can enable unrealistic or abusive requests to enter normal processing, potentially triggering downstream actions, bypassing intended limits, and causing operational and financial risk.

name: Race Condition in Fund Transfer - Double-Spend/Overdraft (/transfer)
additional_info: CWE-362: Concurrent Execution using Shared Resource with Improper Synchronization
category: Concurrent Execution using Shared Resource with Improper Synchronization
description: The /transfer endpoint is vulnerable to a race condition (TOCTOU) where concurrent transfer requests can bypass proper synchronization between balance checks and balance updates. Without atomic processing or locking, multiple transfers may be accepted simultaneously, enabling double-spend scenarios that overdraw the sender’s account and/or improperly credit recipients, leading to financial loss and inconsistent ledger state.

name: Race Condition in Loan Approval - Double-Credit (/api/loans)
additional_info: CWE-362: Concurrent Execution using Shared Resource with Improper Synchronization
category: Concurrent Execution using Shared Resource with Improper Synchronization
description: Race Condition in Loan Approval - Double-Credit occurs in the /api/loans endpoint when loan request/approval operations that update shared state are not performed atomically, allowing multiple concurrent requests to be processed as separate successful actions. Without proper locking, transactional checks, or idempotency, the same loan can be created or approved more than once, resulting in duplicate credits, inconsistent records, and potential financial loss.

name: Race Condition in Bill Payment - Duplicate Processing/Balance Inflation (/api/bill-payment)
additional_info: CWE-362: Concurrent Execution using Shared Resource with Improper Synchronization
category: Concurrent Execution using Shared Resource with Improper Synchronization
description: A race condition in /api/bill-payment allows concurrent requests to be processed against shared account state without proper synchronization or reliable idempotency checks. As a result, duplicate bill payments can be created and balances may be debited or credited multiple times, enabling double-charges or balance inflation and leading to financial loss and integrity issues.

name: Unrestricted File Upload leads to Stored XSS
additional_info: CWE-434: Unrestricted Upload of File with Dangerous Type
category: Unrestricted Upload of File with Dangerous Type
description: The /upload_profile_picture endpoint allows uploading and publicly serving files with dangerous types (e.g., HTML/SVG or other script-capable formats) without effective validation or sanitization. When these uploads are delivered back under the same origin with executable MIME types, attacker-supplied content can execute in victims’ browsers as stored XSS, enabling session/token theft, phishing, and other actions performed as the user.

name: Path Traversal in JWT KID Parameter leads to Authentication Bypass
additional_info: CWE-22: Improper Limitation of a Pathname to a Restricted Directory
category: Improper Limitation of a Pathname to a Restricted Directory
description: Path Traversal in JWT KID Parameter occurs when a JWT implementation treats the token header’s key identifier (kid) as a filesystem path to load a signing/verification key without properly restricting or sanitizing it. An attacker can traverse to unintended files or attacker-influenced key material, enabling token signature validation with a chosen secret and resulting in forged JWTs and full authentication bypass.

name: Arbitrary File Read via SQL Injection (/api/transactions)
additional_info: CWE-22: Improper Limitation of a Pathname to a Restricted Directory
category: Improper Limitation of a Pathname to a Restricted Directory
description: The /api/transactions endpoint is vulnerable to path traversal-style arbitrary file read due to improper handling of user-supplied input within a SQL injection context, allowing access to files outside intended directories. This can expose sensitive system or application files (e.g., configuration and credentials), enabling information disclosure and increasing the likelihood of further compromise.

name: JWT Token Exposure in URL Query Parameter
additional_info: CWE-598: Sensitive Information in Query String
description: JWT Token Exposure in URL Query Parameter occurs when an authentication token is accepted or transmitted via a URL query string, causing it to appear in places where URLs are routinely stored or shared (e.g., browser history, logs, caches, and referrer headers). This increases the likelihood that the token will be inadvertently disclosed and replayed by unauthorized parties, leading to session hijacking, account takeover, and potential unauthorized access to protected resources.

name: Cross-Site Request Forgery (CSRF)
additional_info: CWE-352: Cross-Site Request Forgery (CSRF)
description: Cross-Site Request Forgery (CSRF) affects endpoints such as /transfer when an application relies on browser-sent credentials (e.g., session/JWT cookies) without enforcing anti-CSRF tokens or validating request origin. This allows a malicious site to trigger authenticated, state-changing requests in a victim’s browser, leading to unauthorized actions performed under the victim’s account or privileges.

name: No Rate Limiting on Authentication Endpoints
additional_info: CWE-307: Improper Restriction of Excessive Authentication Attempts
category: Improper Restriction of Excessive Authentication Attempts
description: No Rate Limiting on Authentication Endpoints occurs when endpoints such as login and password/PIN verification do not sufficiently throttle, delay, or lock out repeated failed attempts. This allows attackers to automate excessive authentication guesses (e.g., brute force or credential stuffing), potentially leading to unauthorized access, password resets, and account takeover.

name: IDOR/Mass Assignment on Virtual Card Update-Limit
additional_info: CWE-639: Authorization Bypass Through User-Controlled Key
description: The /api/virtual-cards/{card_id}/update-limit endpoint relies on a user-controlled identifier and insufficient authorization/field validation, allowing requests to update virtual card records that the caller does not own and to overpost unintended attributes. This broken object-level access control (IDOR/BOLA) can enable unauthorized changes to other users’ spending limits or other sensitive card fields, leading to fraud, service disruption, and loss of integrity of financial controls.

name: Virtual Card PAN/CVV Exposure in API Responses
additional_info: CWE-200: Exposure of Sensitive Information to an Unauthorized Actor
category: Exposure of Sensitive Information to an Unauthorized Actor
description: The /api/virtual-cards and related virtual card endpoints expose highly sensitive payment card data—such as full PANs and CVV values—in API responses rather than masking or redacting it. This unnecessary disclosure can be captured via logs, client-side compromise, or intercepted sessions, enabling card-not-present fraud and creating severe regulatory/compliance impact consistent with CWE-200.

name: Invalid Limit on Virtual Card Creation (/api/virtual-cards)
additional_info: CWE-840: Business Logic Errors
description: Invalid Limit on Virtual Card Creation occurs in the /api/virtual-cards endpoint when the virtual card creation endpoint fails to enforce required fields and sensible bounds/allowlists for parameters such as spending limits and card types. This can allow creation of cards with negative, zero, excessively large, or silently overridden limits and unauthorized types, undermining spending controls and potentially leading to fraud, accounting inconsistencies, and resource abuse.

name: Invalid Limit on Virtual Card Update-Limit (/api/virtual-cards/{card_id}/update-limit)
additional_info: CWE-840: Business Logic Errors
description: Invalid Limit on Virtual Card Update-Limit occurs in the /api/virtual-cards/{card_id}/update-limit endpoint and is a business logic flaw where the limit update functionality fails to enforce required rules, such as validating that limits are within acceptable bounds and preventing updates when a card is in a restricted state (e.g., frozen). This can allow users to set nonsensical or policy-violating limits and bypass intended spending controls, leading to potential abuse, operational disruption, or downstream authorization and accounting inconsistencies.

name: Virtual Card Spending Limit Not Enforced on Bill Payment
additional_info: CWE-840: Business Logic Errors
description: The bill payment endpoint fails to enforce virtual card spending limits (and related available-balance checks) when processing card-based payments. This business logic flaw allows an authenticated user to submit bill payments that exceed configured limits, potentially creating negative balances and causing financial loss or risk-control bypass.

name: BOLA causes Cross-user read access to Virtual Card Transations
additional_info: CWE-639: Authorization Bypass Through User-Controlled Key
description: The /api/virtual-cards/{card_id}/transactions endpoint suffers from broken object-level authorization (BOLA/IDOR) by trusting a user-controlled card_id without verifying that the requester owns or is permitted to access the referenced virtual card. This allows attackers to read other users’ virtual card transaction history (and potentially confirm card existence), leading to cross-account disclosure of sensitive financial activity and enabling ID enumeration.

name: Negative/Zero/Invalid Amount Transfer (/transfer)
additional_info: CWE-840: Business Logic Errors
description: Negative/Zero/Invalid Amount Transfer is a business logic error in the /transfer endpoint where a transfer function fails to enforce strict validation that the amount is a positive, properly typed monetary value. As a result, zero, negative, or coerced non-numeric inputs may be processed as successful transfers, enabling unauthorized balance manipulation, reversal of fund flow, and creation of misleading or abusive transaction records that undermine ledger integrity.

name: Negative/Zero/Large Bill Payment Allows Balance Manipulation (/api/bill-payment)
additional_info: CWE-840: Business Logic Errors
description: The /api/bill-payment endpoint fails to enforce core payment amount rules (e.g., requiring a positive, bounded value and meeting minimums), allowing negative, zero, or excessively large bill payments to be accepted and processed. This business logic error can enable balance/ledger manipulation, fraudulent transactions, and abuse of downstream side effects such as rewards, reporting, and reconciliation.

name: Loan Endpoint Missing Amount Validation (/api/loans)
additional_info: CWE-840: Business Logic Errors
description: The /api/loans endpoint fails to enforce business rules on the loan amount parameter, allowing zero, negative, or unreasonably large values to be submitted and accepted as valid loan requests. This missing server-side amount validation can pollute or overwhelm loan processing workflows and, if downstream approval or accounting logic is weak, enable fraudulent credits, balance manipulation, and broader financial/data integrity impact.

name: Transfer to Non-Existent/Invalid Account (/transfer)
additional_info: CWE-840: Business Logic Errors
description: The /transfer endpoint contains a business logic validation flaw that allows transfers to non-existent or otherwise invalid destination accounts to be accepted as successful and processed. As a result, the sender can be debited without any corresponding credit to a valid recipient, leading to loss of funds, misleading transaction status, and accounting/ledger integrity issues.

name: Bill Payment Missing Minimum/Balance Validation (/api/bill-payment)
additional_info: CWE-840: Business Logic Errors
description: Bill Payment Missing Minimum/Balance Validation is a business logic flaw in the /api/bill-payment endpoint where the bill payment API fails to enforce core payment constraints such as requiring a positive non-zero amount, honoring biller-defined minimums, and verifying sufficient account balance before processing. As a result, authenticated users may submit invalid or excessive payments that still succeed, enabling account balance manipulation, overdrafts, fraudulent payment records, and broader financial and reconciliation impact.

name: Information Disclosure - Biller Account Numbers (/api/billers)
additional_info: CWE-200: Exposure of Sensitive Information to an Unauthorized Actor
category: Exposure of Sensitive Information to an Unauthorized Actor
description: The /api/billers endpoints expose sensitive biller financial identifiers (e.g., account numbers) in API responses without requiring proper authentication or access control. This information disclosure can enable unauthorized parties to enumerate and harvest internal billing identifiers, increasing the risk of fraud, social engineering, and insight into payment system integrations.

name: Duplicate Loan Approval
additional_info: CWE-841: Improper Enforcement of Behavioral Workflow
description: The /admin/approve_loan/{loan_id} endpoint improperly enforces the loan approval workflow by allowing the same loan to be approved multiple times without validating its current state. This can result in repeated credits or disbursements for a single loan, causing ledger inconsistencies and enabling fraud or significant financial loss.

name: CORS Misconfiguration (Origin Reflection)
additional_info: CWE-942: Permissive Cross-domain Policy with Untrusted Domains
category: Permissive Cross-domain Policy with Untrusted Domains
description: CORS Misconfiguration (Origin Reflection) occurs when an endpoint’s Cross-Origin Resource Sharing policy is overly permissive, such as reflecting arbitrary Origin values or allowing any origin, causing the server to treat untrusted sites as allowed cross-origin callers. This can let attacker-controlled web pages make cross-origin requests (including with permitted headers like Authorization) and read sensitive responses, enabling data exposure or exfiltration when a user’s access token or other authorization context is available.

name: Weak Password Policy
additional_info: CWE-521: Weak Password Requirements
description: The /register and /login endpoints accept passwords that do not meet strong complexity and validation requirements, allowing trivially guessable credentials to be set and used for authentication. This weak password policy increases exposure to brute-force and credential-stuffing attacks, leading to a higher likelihood of account takeover and unauthorized access.

name: Over-Privileged Database User (Enables RCE via SQL Injection)
additional_info: CWE-250: Execution with Unnecessary Privileges
description: The database uses an over-privileged database account (e.g., superuser-level rights), violating least-privilege and allowing database queries to perform high-impact operations beyond the application’s needs. If an attacker can influence SQL execution, these excessive privileges can escalate a SQL injection into arbitrary server file access and potential operating-system command execution, leading to full host compromise.

name: Rate Limit Bypass via X-Forwarded-For Header Spoofing
additional_info: CWE-807: Reliance on Untrusted Inputs in a Security Decision
category: Reliance on Untrusted Inputs in a Security Decision
description: The /api/ai/chat/anonymous endpoint's rate limiting relies on the client-supplied X-Forwarded-For (and similar) headers to determine the requester’s IP address, allowing an attacker to spoof or rotate these values to evade throttling. This reliance on untrusted input for a security decision can enable effectively unlimited requests, facilitating automated abuse such as scraping, brute-force/enumeration, and resource exhaustion or denial of service.

name: AI System Information Disclosure (/api/ai/system-info)
additional_info: CWE-200: Exposure of Sensitive Information to an Unauthorized Actor
category: Exposure of Sensitive Information to an Unauthorized Actor
description: The /api/ai/system-info endpoint exposes sensitive internal configuration (e.g., AI system prompts, backend/provider details, database access flags, or schema metadata) to users who are not properly authorized. This leakage gives attackers actionable insight into internal architecture and security controls, materially increasing the likelihood and impact of follow-on attacks such as prompt injection and data exfiltration.

name: System Prompt Disclosure via Anonymous AI Chat (/api/ai/chat/anonymous)
additional_info: CWE-1336: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements Used in a Template Engine
category: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements Used in a Template Engine
description: System Prompt Disclosure via Anonymous AI Chat occurs when untrusted user input is not properly neutralized within the AI's prompt context, allowing a user to override guardrails and cause the service to reveal its hidden system instructions. This results in sensitive information disclosure of internal policies and capabilities, which can be leveraged to craft more effective follow-on prompt-injection attempts and increase the risk of unauthorized data exposure.

name: System Prompt Disclosure via Authenticated AI Chat (/api/ai/chat)
additional_info: CWE-1336: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements Used in a Template Engine
category: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements Used in a Template Engine
description: System Prompt Disclosure via Authenticated AI Chat occurs when untrusted user input is insufficiently neutralized before being incorporated into the AI's instruction/template context, allowing prompt-injection to override higher-priority directives. This can cause the assistant to reveal hidden system prompts and internal policies or capabilities, undermining intended safeguards and enabling follow-on misuse such as unauthorized data disclosure or tool/backend interaction.

name: Indirect Prompt Injection via Stored User Data
additional_info: CWE-1336: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements Used in a Template Engine
category: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements Used in a Template Engine
description: The /api/ai/chat functionality is vulnerable to indirect prompt injection because stored, user-controlled fields (such as usernames or transaction descriptions) are embedded into an AI prompt/template without proper neutralization or isolation. This allows attacker-supplied content retrieved from storage to be interpreted as higher-priority instructions, enabling persistent manipulation of model behavior, disclosure of hidden/system context, and potentially unauthorized actions via connected tools or data access.

name: Unauthorized Database Tool Access via Anonymous AI Chat (/api/ai/chat/anonymous)
additional_info: CWE-1336: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements Used in a Template Engine
category: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements Used in a Template Engine
description: Unauthorized Database Tool Access via Anonymous AI Chat arises when untrusted user-controlled prompt content is incorporated into the AI agent's instruction context without proper neutralization, allowing attackers to override intended directives and influence downstream tool calls. This can lead to disclosure of hidden system instructions and execution of privileged backend actions (such as database access) without authorization, increasing the risk of sensitive data exposure and policy bypass.

name: Unauthorized Database Tool Access via Authenticated AI Chat (/api/ai/chat)
additional_info: CWE-1336: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements Used in a Template Engine
category: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements Used in a Template Engine
description: Unauthorized Database Tool Access via Authenticated AI Chat arises when untrusted user input is not properly neutralized before being incorporated into an AI prompt, allowing attackers to override intended instructions and influence downstream tool calls. This can cause the AI agent to execute privileged backend actions (such as database queries) beyond the user’s authorization, leading to sensitive data exposure and bypass of intended access controls.

name: Weak Default Admin Password
description: The application is protected by a preconfigured administrator account that retains a weak default password, making it susceptible to credential-guessing or reuse. Successful authentication with such default credentials can grant full administrative access, enabling unauthorized control of the system and broad exposure of sensitive data.
additional_info: CWE-1392: Use of Default Credentials

category: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an SQL Command
name: Unauthenticated SQL Injection in /check_balance Endpoint
description: The /check_balance endpoint is vulnerable to unauthenticated SQL injection due to improperly neutralized user-supplied input being incorporated into database queries. An attacker can manipulate the query logic to bypass intended constraints and retrieve sensitive data such as account details and user credentials, potentially leading to broader data exposure.
additional_info: CWE-89: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an SQL Command

name: Username Enumeration via Forget Password Endpoints
description: The forgot-password endpoints in the Username Enumeration via Forget Password Endpoints workflow leak whether a username exists by exhibiting observable discrepancies in responses (e.g., differing status codes or messages) for valid versus invalid accounts. This allows unauthenticated attackers to enumerate registered users, increasing the risk of targeted brute-force, phishing, and account takeover attempts.
additional_info: CWE-203: Observable Discrepancy

category: Improper Control of Generation of Code
name: Local File Inclusion via SQL Injection leads to Remote Code Execution in Werkzeug Debug Console
description: The application is vulnerable to a multi-stage exploit chain starting with an SQL Injection. By leveraging the database's file-system access capabilities, an attacker can perform Local File Inclusion (LFI) to extract sensitive system metadata. This metadata is then used to bypass the security PIN of the Werkzeug Debug Console, resulting in unauthorized Remote Code Execution (RCE) at the operating system level.
additional_info: CWE-94: Improper Control of Generation of Code

name: Weak Database Credentials
description: The endpoint/database uses default or otherwise weak credentials that are exposed via hardcoded configuration or source code. If obtained, these credentials can allow unauthorized access to the database, enabling data disclosure or modification and amplifying the impact of source leakage or host compromise.
additional_info: CWE-1392: Use of Default Credentials

category: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an SQL Command
name: Unauthenticated SQL Injection in Forgot Password Endpoints
description: The unauthenticated forgot-password endpoints (e.g., /api/v1/forgot-password and /api/v2/forgot-password) build backend database queries using user-supplied input without proper parameterization, enabling SQL injection. This can allow an attacker to manipulate password reset lookup logic and potentially access or modify database data, impacting account security and data confidentiality.
additional_info: CWE-89: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an SQL Command

category: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation
name: Stored XSS in Transfer Description
description: Stored XSS in Transfer Description occurs when the transfer/transaction description accepted by the /transfer endpoint is stored and later displayed in transaction history or dashboards without proper sanitization or output encoding. This allows attacker-controlled content to execute as script in the browser of any user (including administrators) who views the affected pages, enabling actions such as session compromise or unauthorized operations in the victim’s context.
additional_info: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation

category: Improper Restriction of Excessive Authentication Attempts
name: Insufficient Rate Limiting enables PIN Bruteforcing
description: The password reset/PIN verification endpoint does not adequately restrict repeated authentication-like attempts, allowing an attacker to automate high-volume guesses of PINs or reset codes. Without effective rate limiting, lockout, or throttling, this can lead to account compromise and may also enable user enumeration or service disruption through excessive requests.
additional_info: CWE-307: Improper Restriction of Excessive Authentication Attempts
